items.mistake: Fitting PCA on the full dataset including test rows before evaluation
items.fix: Fit PCA inside a pipeline on training data only; call transform on validation and test sets with the fitted mean and components.
items.why: Test-informed components leak distribution information and inflate reported performance.
items.example: Use `Pipeline([('scaler', StandardScaler()), ('pca', PCA(10)), ('clf', LogisticRegression())])` with `fit` on X_train only.

items.mistake: Skipping centering (or letting PCA fit on uncentered data unintentionally)
items.fix: Subtract column means before covariance/SVD, or use sklearn PCA which centers by default during fit.
items.why: PCA directions are defined around the mean; uncentered data shifts geometry and misaligns variance maximization.
items.example: Always compare `X - X.mean(axis=0)` before manual SVD; sklearn `PCA` centers internally on fit.

items.mistake: Choosing k solely because '2D plots are pretty'
items.fix: Use cumulative explained variance, scree elbow, reconstruction error, or downstream cross-validated task performance to pick k.
items.why: Two components may retain too little variance for modeling or too much noise for denoising.
items.example: If cumulative explained variance at k=2 is 0.55, consider k=10 if 0.95 is needed for reconstruction.

items.mistake: Mixing up PCA on covariance vs correlation when features have different units
items.fix: Standardize (zero mean, unit variance) when scales differ and you want equal feature weight; keep raw covariance PCA when magnitude matters.
items.why: Income in dollars can dominate age in years on the first component unless you standardize.
items.example: Wrap `StandardScaler()` before `PCA()` in a pipeline for mixed-scale census columns.
